title: Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)

compliance.assurance_rationale: SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — monitoring changes to the monitoring stack itself is explicitly a SWIFT CSP control; the Splunk conf-change UC is the direct evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SWIFT CSP 6.4 (Logging and monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-13.1.36: Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit).

spl:
index=_audit action IN ("edit_*","update")
| search file_path="*.conf" OR object_path="*local*"
| table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
| sort -_time
description: Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.
value: Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.
implementation: Map `action` types to your Splunk version. Alert on changes outside change windows. Route to SecOps for prod SH/CM.
visualization: Timeline (config changes), Table (recent edits by user).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Audit trail.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_audit` (`action` for config changes).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map `action` types to your Splunk version. Alert on changes outside change windows. Route to SecOps for prod SH/CM.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit action IN ("edit_*","update")
| search file_path="*.conf" OR object_path="*local*"
| table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)** — Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_audit` (`action` for config changes). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Audit trail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)**): table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (config changes), Table (recent edits by user).
grandmaExplanation: Unauthorized edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast — so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
